title: Oldenlandia rugosa
taxonomy: Oldenlandia rugosa is classified within the family Rubiaceae, which is a large and diverse family of flowering plants commonly known as the coffee or madder family. The genus Oldenlandia is a part of this family and includes several species of small annual or perennial herbs. Oldenlandia rugosa specifically is a species within this genus, characterized by its rugose or wrinkled leaves. The classification of Oldenlandia rugosa is further supported by its morphology and genetic characteristics. This classification is consistent with the most recent taxonomy and systematic studies on the Rubiaceae family.
uses: Oldenlandia rugosa has been traditionally used in various cultures for its medicinal properties. It is said to have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, making it useful for wound healing and skin infections. The plant is also believed to have antioxidant and anti-cancer properties, although more research is needed to confirm these claims. In addition, Oldenlandia rugosa is thought to have antiviral and antifungal properties, which could potentially be used to treat various diseases. Further studies are necessary to fully understand the potential medicinal uses of Oldenlandia rugosa.

intro: Oldenlandia rugosa, also known as Oldenlandia, is a species of flowering plant in the family Rubiaceae, native to tropical regions of Asia and Australia. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, with reported health benefits including the treatment of fever, inflammation, and respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs, due to its anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties. The bioactive constituents of Oldenlandia rugosa include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and iridoid glycosides, which are responsible for its medicinal activities. In traditional Chinese medicine, the plant is commonly prepared into teas, infusions, and tinctures, and is also used in combination with other herbs to treat a range of health conditions.
